verdict_reasoning: VEGFA (VEGF-A) is a secreted growth factor with no transmembrane domain, no GPI anchor, and no intrinsic membrane anchor of its own. It is produced as a signal-peptide–bearing homodimer that is constitutively secreted into the extracellular space, where it binds its receptors (VEGFR1, VEGFR2, NRP1) on other cells. Pre-`no` checklist: (1) Cell-state-induced surface display — no documented gene-specific outer-leaflet ecto-display on tumor or dying cells for VEGFA protein body itself; the upregulation in tumors is secretion-level upregulation, not surface anchoring. (2) Tissue-restricted surface display — the VEGF189 isoform binds tightly to cell-surface HSPGs and ECM, but this is reversible heparan-sulfate binding that stays in equilibrium with the soluble pool (Eylea blocks it, confirming it is accessible as a soluble pool); this is not wash-resistant anchoring to a TM partner. (3) Lysosomal exocytosis — VEGFA is not a lysosomal TM protein. (4) Dual localization / PM cycling — no documented stable minority PM pool via its own anchor; engineered membrane-tethered variants are artificial constructs, not endogenous biology. (5) Stable surface attachment via covalent/wash-resistant TM-partner tethering — no evidence. All therapeutic programs (bevacizumab, aflibercept, ranibizumab, etc.) target the circulating/soluble pool, not a cell-surface–anchored form. Verdict: secreted_only.
